message: Coffee's bad today. Anyway - jalapeño corn dog bites. We're doing it.
Yeah, the coffee machine's been weird all week - but okay, so you're feeling really solid about the jalapeño direction?
The heat's the easy part - what makes this work is the textural contrast, the way the crisp exterior gives you that snap before the cheese pulls. That's the story worth telling.

Marcus just made a really smart observation about texture and narrative - exactly the kind of thing that makes her trust his instincts even when his drafts run long. She wants to say "yes, that's it exactly, let's build around that" but she's also slightly worried he'll turn it into 800 words about cheese pull dynamics. She needs to validate him, lock in the direction, and move forward without sounding like she's micromanaging. Marcus nailed it - the snap-to-pull moment is what people will actually remember and want to make again.
Yeah - so we're anchoring the whole thing there, which means the copy has to earn that moment instead of explaining it to death.
He's right about the story - crisp snap matters more than heat. Let's build it that way. See you tomorrow.
